_comment: THE LIBRARY (ship-guide goal): the user guide INSIDE the game — every book a focused instruction manual, every book a ROW. ProtoBookPanel renders these; the safehouse BOOKSHELF opens the library; book_* items (items.json) open a single book from the pack. Pages are plain text, ~10 short lines each, bind names written as the DEFAULT keys (F11 rebinds).

books.title: DRIVER'S HANDBOOK
pages:
GETTING MOVING

Walk to any rig and press E to take the wheel.
W is the gas, S the brake/reverse, A/D steer.
SPACE is the HANDBRAKE — at speed it breaks the rear loose into a controlled drift.
Stand on the gas from a standstill and the wheels light up in a BURNOUT.
E steps back out. A passenger can HOLD E to slide over and take the wheel.

THE FIVE PARTS

Every rig has an ENGINE, TIRES, BATTERY, FUEL TANK and CHASSIS — and you FEEL each one fail:
engine misfires, steering slop, flickering lights, a leaking tank.
Salvaged CAR PARTS repair the worst component. A TIRE KIT patches rubber.
A breached tank plus a critical chassis is a fire spiral — get OUT.
Wrecks always burn to a husk. Nothing drives home from that.

FUEL & THE ROAD

The gauge drains as you drive; a JERRY CAN pours +40 fuel into a rig within reach.
Dirt is slower than asphalt — tires decide how much (knobby buggy barely cares, highway van suffers).
Weather matters: rain kills grip, dust kills the headlight cone, heat cooks engines.
In the far wilderness the land ROLLS — heavy states like Colorado are real climbs.
RACING: find a race board, cycle a course with E, and beat your own ghost.

books.title: ON FOOT: THE MOVESET
pages:
MOVING

WASD moves, SHIFT sprints (stamina), CTRL holds a CROUCH — quiet, small, ×0.55 noise.
Sprint + tap CTRL = a SLIDE. SPACE is the combat DIVE.
Walk into water and you wade, then swim. Stamina gone mid-swim is how people drown.
B raises the binoculars. T waits (the clock sprints while you hold still).

UNARMED

LMB tap throws a PUNCH — taps chain into a jab-jab-cross COMBO.
Hold LMB to SHOVE. Sprint + tap = a TACKLE that puts them on the ground.
MARTIAL ARTS levels by doing: lv2 the finisher becomes a KICK, lv4 a shove at
grapple range is a THROW, lv6 punching a downed body is a FINISHER (×3).
Press U to see the whole skill tree light up as you earn it.

ARMED

` cycles weapons; 1/2/3 jump to a slot. R reloads. G throws a grenade.
Right-stick or mouse aims; melee respects walls — no cutting through brick.
Every kill leaves a BODY, not a crate: walk up, E loots the corpse.
Bodies rot away — a picked-clean one goes fast. Cars are weapons too:
anything on the road can be run down. Including you.

books.title: THE PACK: DOGS
pages:
TAKING ONE IN

Strays roam the wastes. E adopts one; P pets it (do this often — the BOND is real).
Feed it dried meat with E. The bond climbs STRAY → COMPANION → PARTNER → SOULBOUND,
and the prompt wears the tier so you always know where you stand.
KINSHIP (your skill) makes every command land faster and taming cheaper.

THE WHISTLE (C)

×1 HEEL — come to me.  ×2 GUARD — hold this spot.
×3 SEEK — sniff out the nearest cache (Hunters can DIG buried ones).
HOLD — SIC: take something down.  ×4 SHIELD — SOULBOUND only: locks to your hip.
Dogs JUMP fences on their own and POUNCE when sicced.

THE DOG'S EYE

Send a PARTNER or SOULBOUND dog seeking and it CARRIES YOUR EYE:
the screen splits as it ranges out — you on one side, the dog's ground on the other.
The tighter the bond, the farther it ranges before the split (SOULBOUND sees farthest).
The eye folds shut when the dog comes off the search.

DOGS ARE MORTAL. Down beside one with a bandage in time, or you dig a grave,
keep the collar, and the kennel wall remembers the name.

books.title: HOME & SURVIVAL
pages:
THE SAFEHOUSE

Meridian's two-story house is yours: supply chest inside, TV in the corner,
drone dock and BUILD BOARD by the door, kennel across the street.
The build board spends scrap: WALLS I–III (raids test them), garage, kennel,
workbench, bed. F drops a beacon pointing home. M is the map; N cycles waypoints.

THE CLOCK & THE BODY

A full day passes in 24 real minutes; the moon decides how dark night gets.
HUNGER drains on the clock — meat, cans, camp-stove meals fix it. Water and
coffee refill stamina; whiskey trades stress for a little torso.
Wounds land on six body parts and TAX you: a bad leg limps, a bad arm shakes your aim.
Bandages patch the worst; a medkit treats everything.

SAVING & GOING DOWN

F5 quick-saves, F9 loads. One file carries the world: dogs, home, ledger, clock.
Going down is NOT the end: you wake at the safehouse, mended — but the
wasteland takes a cut of scrap and scrip, and your rig stays where it fell.
Dogs are the permadeath. Guard them like it.
K opens the character sheet — the whole world, narrated. U is the skill tree.

books.title: GADGETS & SIGNALS
pages:
THE DRONE

USE a scout drone from your pack (TAB) and it patrols overhead, pinging threats.
Use it AGAIN to TAKE THE STICK: your body stands still while you fly with WASD —
and the screen SPLITS as the bird ranges out. You can't switch it off mid-air;
E brings it in to LAND. Attacked while flying? It HOVERS and you snap back.
Docks recharge a drone in a QUARTER OF A DAY — or T-wait the charge away.
The military's HUMVEE carries its own drone bay on the deck.

EYES & EARS

SURVEILLANCE CAMERA: plant it watching a road; V cycles the corner window
to its feed (plant several — V steps camera to camera). E packs it up.
MOTION SENSOR: plant it on an approach; it PINGS you when anything crosses.
WALKIE-TALKIE: key it and LISTEN — movement bleeds through the static
with a compass direction. Dead air means you're alone. Mostly.

RADIO & TV

O opens the RADIO: drag the dial across the FM band — stations lock, static
hisses between them; preset chips jump straight to a frequency. L is next-station,
comma/period the volume. Y SCANS the airwaves for distress calls and warnings.
The safehouse TV: E to sit down, arrows change channels, the slider sets volume.
Five channels broadcast — films, trailers, the EBS when the world turns.

books.title: THE CAROUSEL
pages:
WHAT IT IS

Ten buried military gates ring the country — THE CAROUSEL. A lit gate JUMPS you
across the map. The catch: every gate is a DUNGEON first. Get close and its
OCCUPIERS wake up. The terminal names the price: PURGE the squatters,
buy or earn LAUNCH CODES, socket POWER (jerry cans, power cells).

THE SPIN-UP

Pay the price and the ring starts to SPIN — loud. Waves come to shut it down.
Hold the room until the ring burns teal: the gate is ACTIVE, a reward chest
materializes, and a PORTAL grows on the platform.
Activate the portal and the computer counts you down — ten, nine, eight —
then THE DIAL rolls and you're somewhere else. E at the terminal jumps quietly too.

KEEPING IT

Lit nodes get BESIEGED — relieve them before the deadline or they go dark.
NODE GARAGES: a rig parked at a gate travels with the network.
With Cheyenne's TARGETING CORE the dial stops being roulette:
the jump follows YOUR map course. That's the endgame of the ring.

books.title: THE DIVIDED STATES
pages:
THE COUNTRY

The USA, compressed 60:1 — four hours of real driving crosses it in four minutes.
Every state has a RULER and the border reads your ledger: heroes get welcomes,
problems get BOUNTY HUNTERS. Welcome signs tell you whose law you're under.
RESPECT is per-faction and per-state: prices, standing, and refusals all move with it.

THE CIRCUIT

The loop that keeps you alive: SCAVENGE a cache → UPGRADE at the workbench →
PUSH farther out → light a NODE. The HUD pips track the cycle; finishing one pays.
Y catches radio: distress caches, trade runs, howler warnings.
Weekly, somewhere, a STATE GOES TO WAR — the map is not decoration.

THE NIGHT

Night shrinks what you can see — that's the danger, not a number.
HOWLERS hunt in packs with roles: circlers pin you, chargers come straight,
a SCREAMER calls more. Headlights scare them; so do flares.
LURKERS wait indoors. The walkie hears them moving before you see them.
Respect the dark or feed it.

books.title: POCKET GAME DECK FIELD GUIDE
pages:
USING THE HANDHELD

Find the POCKET GAME DECK in the safehouse supply chest. Open TAB, select it, and USE it. The road keeps moving while you play: hunger, weather, danger, and the clock do not pause. ESC or START opens the cartridge pause page; B / Circle returns to the little physical screen without killing the run. POWER ends the cartridge. HELP and CONTROLS always show your live keyboard, mouse, and pad bindings.

WASTE HEAP

Slide the whole salvage tray with arrows, WASD, D-pad, or left stick. Equal-numbered parts fuse once per move and a new part arrives after a successful slide. Build the largest reactor core before no move remains. Corners are stable but can trap low parts; preserve one clean descending edge. The primary record is SCORE, then HIGHEST PART. A challenge copies the seed, so both players receive the same luck.

RADWORM

Steer the Continuity routing worm through bright lost packets. Each packet lengthens the tail and raises the score. Touching the border, firewall debris, or your own tail ends the route. Plan two turns ahead and keep the center open; circling the perimeter too early removes escape lanes. Primary record: LENGTH. Tiebreaker: SURVIVAL TIME. Remote play is an asynchronous seed challenge.

DEAD GROUND

Reveal a square with CONFIRM / primary click; FLAG suspected ordnance with CANCEL / secondary click. A number counts mines in the eight touching squares. Empty ground opens as a flood. Begin from certain zeros, mark only forced mines, and use the remaining-mine counter before guessing. Primary record is lowest CLEAR TIME; ERRORS break ties. The board seed is preserved for fair challenges.

PACK RAT

Push every cargo crate onto a marked evacuation bay. You can push one crate but never pull it, and two crates cannot be pushed together. Before each move, check whether a crate would enter a non-goal corner or a wall pocket; either can make the level impossible. Work from the deepest bay outward so finished crates do not block the aisle. Record: LEVELS cleared, then fewer MOVES.

BUNKER BREAKER

Move the paddle horizontally and keep the breaching slug in play. The return angle changes with the part of the paddle you strike: edges send it wide, center sends it straight. Clear every armor brick to descend a bunker layer. Preserve lives by meeting the ball instead of chasing it late. Primary record: SCORE. Tiebreaker: LAYERS breached. The portrait screen is intentional.

LAST MILE

Steer with left/right, accelerate and brake with the displayed racer bindings. Thread civilian traffic, clip checkpoints in order, and finish the courier course with as few contacts as possible. Smooth steering holds speed; panic corrections cost more time than a brief lift. Learn traffic rhythm before taking the inside line. Primary record is lowest COURSE TIME; CLEAN LAPS settle equal runs.

IRON DOME

Aim the ground battery with pointer or stick and fire interceptors into incoming missile trails. An interceptor bursts where you trigger it; one expanding blast can chain nearby warheads. Protect the cities, conserve shots, and detonate ahead of clustered targets rather than directly on one. Empty silos do not reload mid-wave. Primary record: SCORE. Tiebreaker: CITIES SAVED.

FALL LINE

Rotate and pulse the lander's thrusters to settle on the marked pad. Gravity never stops working; kill horizontal drift early, then descend on short controlled burns. Landing too fast, too tilted, or outside the pad destroys the craft. Fuel remaining matters, but a gentle touchdown matters first. Primary record: LANDING RATING. Tiebreaker: FUEL.

TILT SALVAGE

Use left and right flippers to keep the steel salvage ball alive. Launch with the shown action, nudge sparingly, and shoot lit lanes to build multipliers. A late flipper sends the ball sideways; an early flipper sends it high. Repeated nudges trigger a tilt and kill the table. Primary record: SCORE. Tiebreaker: JACKPOTS.

RELAY BLOOM

Rotate relay tiles until power forms an unbroken path from the source to every bloom. CONFIRM turns the selected tile; pointer play can select directly. Build outward from fixed terminals and use edge constraints before touching the ambiguous center. Completing several relays without a correction grows the combo. Primary record: SCORE. Tiebreaker: MAX COMBO.

books.title: SAFEHOUSE CONSOLE STRATEGY ANNUAL
pages:
USING THE CONSOLE

The amber set beside the safehouse TV is an ordinary E interactable. All cartridges use one 16:9 screen, shell, controls page, score ledger, save block, and network seam. Local players must be physically near the set and claim separate pads. Online players join from another terminal. Closing the fullscreen bezel returns your body but leaves the cartridge alive on the set. POWER ends it.

CROWN OF ASH

This is complete chess under wasteland colors. Select a piece, select its destination, and protect your crown. Castling, en passant, promotion, check, checkmate, stalemate, repetition, insufficient material, and the fifty-move draw all apply. Captures play a battle vignette but never change the legal result. Develop pieces before raiding, keep the king sheltered, and inspect every enemy reply. Records count WINS, then CLOCK time.

DIAL TANKS

Drive and aim independently. Shells ricochet off hard walls, mines punish repeated routes, and the last working tank wins. Use cover to split enemy sightlines, then move before return fire lands. Local and online rounds use the same authoritative snapshot. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: KILLS.

RED SKY

Two artillery crews trade shots across ruined terrain. Set angle and power, account for wind, then fire. Explosions deform cover and can expose a better line for the next shell. A ranging shot that misses safely is often better than gambling maximum power. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: DAMAGE dealt.

BLACK ORBIT

Pilot a salvage skiff through a wraparound asteroid field. Turn, thrust, aim, and fire independently; inertia persists when thrust stops. Break rock, collect salvage, and deny the rival without drifting into debris. Short burns are safer than constant thrust. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: SALVAGE.

GRIDBREACH

Plant charges through the maze, retreat behind hard cover, and use blast chains to cut routes or trap rivals. Fire travels in straight lanes until a wall stops it; upgrades extend reach and charge count. Never enter a pocket without an exit timer in mind. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: SURVIVAL TIME.

RUSTBALL

Body the iron ball through the rival gate. Momentum transfers through every hit, so approach at an angle that sends the ball where you want after contact. One player presses, one covers the rebound. Boost spent on a lost ball is boost unavailable for the save. Primary record: GOALS. Tiebreaker: SAVES.

FUEL RUN

Race armed scrap cars around the refinery, collect the fuel cell, and return it to your depot. The carrier is heavier and visible to everyone. Teammates should screen intersections while the carrier takes the clean line. Crashes can knock the cell loose. Primary record: CAPTURES. Tiebreaker: fastest LAP.

SKYJOUST

Climb, dive, and lance rival gliders out of the county-fair sky. Speed gives authority but also lengthens every turn. Attack from above, cross once, then regain altitude instead of circling at stall speed. A clean lance hit matters more than grazing fire. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: KNOCKOUTS.

FIGHT NIGHT '99

Move, guard, strike, and commit to specials in a two-dimensional tournament ring. High attacks catch movement; low attacks open a standing guard; throws punish passive blocking. Learn recovery frames and stop mashing into a missed heavy. Best-of rounds support local and online rivals. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: HP REMAINING.

ASHLAND COMMAND

Move squads and armor across a turn-based grid, capture supply points, and break the opposing command unit. Terrain changes defense and movement cost; damaged units still occupy space. Concentrate fire, screen artillery, and deny repairs. The battle is deterministic and supports hot-seat or reliable online turns. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: fewer TURNS.

books.title: RUST RUNNERS MERCENARY MANUAL
pages:
FIELD CONTROLS

Move with WASD / left stick and aim with mouse / right stick. PRIMARY fires, SECONDARY throws or uses the carried tool, JUMP ignites the boots, RELOAD changes magazines, SWAP cycles weapons, and USE handles objectives. The same shared-shooter profile is used by BLACK GRID so muscle memory transfers exactly.

MOVEMENT & JETS

RUST RUNNERS is fast side-view infantry combat: run, jump, crouch, prone, and burn limited jet fuel to cross ledges or change a duel's height. Momentum survives in the air. Feather the jet instead of holding it; a dry pack at the top of an arc leaves no answer to incoming fire.

GUNFIGHTS

Weapons differ in magazine, recoil, projectile speed, and role. Fire short automatic bursts, lead moving targets, and reload behind terrain. Grenades inherit your motion. Health kits and weapon drops are contested resources. Objective wins matter before kill counts.

MODES & RECORDS

Deathmatch, team deathmatch, capture, and hold objectives share one deterministic match contract. Local players use separate pads at the same console; remote peers use the same controls and rules. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: KILLS. All art, maps, names, sounds, and lore are original DRIVN material.

books.title: BLACK GRID CONTINUITY DOCTRINE
pages:
FIELD CONTROLS

Move with WASD / left stick and aim with mouse / right stick. PRIMARY fires, SECONDARY uses the selected equipment, JUMP becomes the infantry maneuver, RELOAD changes magazines, SWAP cycles inventory, and USE enters vehicles or works objectives. This is the exact shared-shooter layout used by RUST RUNNERS.

MASS, POWER, AND LOADOUT

BLACK GRID is a top-down combined-arms battle. Every weapon, cell, tool, and armor plate adds mass; heavy kits accelerate slowly and spend more energy. Power drives equipment, shields, deployables, and some vehicles. Build a role instead of carrying everything.

SQUADS & OBJECTIVES

Fight for generators, relays, depots, and territory rather than wandering for kills. Engineers establish defenses, medics preserve tickets, scouts expose routes, and heavy crews break hard points. Vehicles require space, fuel/power, and infantry support. A squad that shares information beats four excellent loners.

CLEAN-ROOM LINE

No Infantry Online or FreeInfantry client code, server code, maps, zone files, art, audio, names, text, or branding is included. BLACK GRID is original DRIVN code and presentation using only documented and observed player-facing genre mechanics. Primary record: WINS. Tiebreaker: OBJECTIVE SCORE.
